title: Marinated Moroccan Pork Shoulder Couscous Royal
description: Jammy, chewy Moroccan pork shoulder served over fluffy couscous with raisins and almonds, perfect for meal prep.
instructions: Whisk olive oil, spice mix, lemon juice, rosemary, garlic, salt, and pepper to make a paste.
Rub the paste all over the pork shoulder, cover, and marinate in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours, up to overnight.
Place the pork in a roasting pan and roast for 90 minutes, basting every 30 minutes, until the internal temperature reaches 145°F.
Let the pork rest 10 minutes before slicing into thick strips.
Bring 2 cups of water to a boil, stir in couscous, remove from heat, cover, and let steam for 10 minutes.
Fluff the couscous with a fork, then stir in dried fruit, toasted almonds, and chopped parsley.
Arrange sliced pork over the couscous, drizzle with leftover juices, and serve.
Pack portions into airtight containers for meal prep; store in the refrigerator up to 4 days.
